mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that supports equity valuations and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of observations when industrial production accelerates during periods of rising federal funds rates.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling federal funds rates and rising nonfarm payrolls creates a soft-landing environment that boosts risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of rate cuts and payroll gains produces negative average 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent annually signals severe demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of quarters where annualized real GDP growth drops below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indices reflect stronger manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency that improve corporate profit margins and generate positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of periods with rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during a post-shock recovery phase indicates that labor market slack persists despite policy support, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns fail to materialize in more than 65% of observations when unemployment rises during the post-shock recovery regime.
